name: Vadiello - Huevo de San Cosme Trail
name_original: Embalse de Vadiello – Huevo de San Cosme por Ermita de San Cosme y San Damián
description: Hike the Vadiello - Huevo de San Cosme Trail, a moderate 6.7-mile route with unique rock formations and reservoir views in Sierra de Guara.
region_intro: Hiking around Loporzano offers access to diverse natural landscapes within the Huesca province of Aragon, Spain. The region features varied terrain, from the rugged Pre-Pyrenees mountains to verdant valleys and unique rock formations. It is characterized by its proximity to the Sierra de Guara Natural Park, known for dramatic canyons and clear waters, as well as scenic reservoirs like Vadiello. This area provides a range of experiences for outdoor enthusiasts. Best hiking trails around Loporzano The most popular hiking route is Vadiello - Huevo de San Cosme Trail, a 6.7 miles (10.8 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 10 minutes to complete. This route features distinctive rock formations and scenic reservoir views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Garganta del Guatizalema Superior desde el Embalse de Vadiello, a moderate 5.7 miles (9.1 km) path. It winds through canyons and offers views of the Vadiello reservoir. Local hikers also love the Vadiello, San Cosme, and El Huevo Loop, a 12.3 miles (19.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain with reservoir views, often completed in about 5 hours 52 minutes. Hiking around Loporzano is defined by canyons, unique rock formations, and Pre-Pyrenees mountains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Loporzano are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 270 reviews. More than 2,100 hikers have used komoot to explore Loporzano's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of hiking terrain can I expect around Loporzano?
A: Loporzano offers a diverse range of terrain, from the rugged mountains of the Pre-Pyrenees to verdant valleys and unique rock formations. The area is particularly known for its proximity to the Sierra de Guara Natural Park, which features dramatic canyons and clear waters. You'll find trails winding through gorges, past reservoirs, and showcasing distinctive geological features like those seen on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939020323/vadiello-huevo-de-san-cosme-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vadiello - Huevo de San Cosme Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Loporzano?
A: Yes, Loporzano offers a variety of trails for different ability levels. While many routes are moderate, there are at least 3 easy trails available, perfect for beginners or families looking for a more relaxed walk. These routes allow you to enjoy the scenic landscapes without significant challenge.

Q: Can I find challenging hikes near Loporzano?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Loporzano features at least 3 difficult routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e945301764/vadiello-san-cosme-and-el-huevo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vadiello, San Cosme, and El Huevo Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which spans nearly 20 km and involves significant elevation changes, offering a rewarding experience through varied terrain and reservoir views.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Loporzano?
A: Yes, Loporzano has several circular routes. These are great for exploring the area without retracing your steps. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1396833" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vadiello Dam – Esconjuradero loop from Santa Eulalia la Mayor&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate hike with scenic views.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Loporzano?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and interesting landmarks. You can encounter dramatic canyons, unique rock formations, and scenic reservoirs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2981345" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arguis Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5182260" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vadiello Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. There's also the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/348950" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de Bierge waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and historical sites such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5254839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montearagón Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature waterfalls near Loporzano?
A: Yes, you can find waterfalls in the vicinity of Loporzano. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/348950" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de Bierge&lt;/a&gt; is a notable waterfall highlight that can be incorporated into or visited alongside hiking routes in the area, offering a refreshing natural spectacle.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Loporzano?
A: The hiking trails around Loporzano are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 270 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from canyons to reservoir views, and the unique rock formations. The variety of routes catering to different skill levels is also a common highlight.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in the Loporzano area?
A: There are 16 distinct hiking trails available around Loporzano. These routes vary in difficulty, including 3 easy, 10 moderate, and 3 difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable path for every hiker.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Loporzano?
A: Loporzano, being in the Pre-Pyrenees, offers enjoyable hiking for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, especially for longer routes. Winter hiking is possible, but conditions can vary, particularly at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any specific trails that offer views of the Vadiello reservoir?
A: Many trails in the Loporzano area offer stunning views of the Vadiello reservoir. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939020323/vadiello-huevo-de-san-cosme-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vadiello - Huevo de San Cosme Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939026098/garganta-del-guatizalema-superior-desde-el-embalse-de-vadiello" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garganta del Guatizalema Superior desde el Embalse de Vadiello&lt;/a&gt; both feature scenic reservoir vistas as part of their routes.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural attractions near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region combines natural beauty with historical interest. Beyond the trails, you can explore sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5254839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montearagón Castle&lt;/a&gt;, a significant historical landmark. The Sierra de Guara Natural Park, which borders Loporzano, also contains prehistoric caves with ancient paintings, some of which are UNESCO World Heritage sites within the Vero River Cultural Park.
tips: This idyllic and solitary spot formed by the water of the reservoir, the river itself and its ravines and the monumental mallos that surround it, is a must-see for anyone who wants to get to know the Guara Natural Park and enjoy nature and silence. just fifteen minutes from the city of Huesca.

The Hermitage of San Cosme and San Damián is located in an impressive natural setting near the Vadiello reservoir, in the Sierra de Guara. It is known for its beauty and for being part of a hiking route that is suitable for all audiences. The excursion to this hermitage and other nearby ones such as San Urbez, Fuensanta and Virgen de Fabana, is of moderate effort and is perfectly signposted. 🌄😎🚶‍♀️
Very good route to do with children and by bicycle, very exposed to the sun, in hot months not highly recommended

In places like this, priests performed rituals invoking saints to stop violent storms, associated with witches and the devil. The four arches are oriented to the cardinal points to observe the direction in which the storms were moving.

More information: https://www.heraldo.es/noticias/viajes/2022/06/10/esconjuraderos-que-son-estas-construcciones-y-donde-encontrarlas-en-aragon-1580356.html

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Vadiello - Huevo de San Cosme Trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is generally considered a moderate hike. While some sections are suitable for beginners, its varied terrain and approximately 535 meters of elevation gain and loss mean it's best for hikers with some experience and good physical condition.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from the Vadiello reservoir parking area. This is the most common and convenient access point for the route.

faqs.question: Is the trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The Vadiello - Huevo de San Cosme Trail is a circular route, allowing you to experience diverse scenery without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll encounter a tunnel carved into rock, well-marked gravel tracks, and dirt paths winding through Mediterranean scrub. Expect impressive conglomerate rock walls and unique geological formations like the "Huevo de San Cosme".

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best times to visit are generally spring and autumn. Due to the trail's lack of shade, it's highly recommended to avoid hiking during the hot summer months.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Vadiello - Huevo de San Cosme Trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail is generally dog-friendly, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife and varied terrain. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this trail or the Sierra de Guara Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail or to access the Parque Natural de la Sierra y los Cañones de Guara. However, always respect local regulations and signage within the park.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks and viewpoints will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several significant sights. Highlights include the Vadiello Dam, the unique View of the Huevo de San Cosme, and the ancient Hermitage of San Cosme and San Damián. You'll also see the impressive Ligüerri mallos rock formations and enjoy panoramic views of the Vadiello reservoir.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I spot on the trail?
faqs.answer: The area is known for its rupicolous birdlife. Keep an eye out for species such as bearded vultures and griffon vultures soaring above the cliffs.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is officially known as "Vadiello, San Cosme y San Damián - El Huevo de San Cosme" and covers 100% of that path. It also briefly intersects with sections of the "Camino Natural de la Hoya de Huesca" and the "Paso de Nartesa".

faqs.question: Is there an insider tip for hiking this route?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's popularity and the lack of shade, an insider tip is to start your hike early in the morning, especially outside of peak summer, to enjoy cooler temperatures and potentially fewer crowds at key viewpoints.
